creator: dglo@hyde.ssec.wisc.edu
text:
Ernst de Haan wrote:
> Kees Jan,
> 
> > http://www.kjkoster.org/download/mysql-jdbc-mm.diff
> 
> Some comments on the actual patch:
> 
> 1) Please use the unified diff format (diff -u). It's easier to read through.
> 2) PORTREVISION=1? PORTREVISION should only be bumped if the PORTVERSION 
> stays the same, but the contents for the port have changed, so that a 
> portupgrade would install an improved version of the application.
> 3) Perhaps we should rename mm.mysql-2.0.7-bin.jar to mm.mysql-2.0.7.jar? The
> -bin postfix is not really useful, is it?
> 
> In general the changes to the port look very nice indeed.

I'd prefer to have the port built from source, rather than
installing the binary .jar file.  So far, only Ernst and Kees Jan
have replied to my message and both of them have said they don't
use JDBC1.  My port builds a JDBC2 driver. Why do you want to
commit Kees Jan's port?

Version 2.0.8 is out, the port should use that rather than
2.0.7.

Rather than creating a new mysql-jdbc-mm2 port which would be
replaced by a mysql-jdbc-mm3 port when 3.x comes out, why not
move the current port to mysql-jdbc-mm1 and update
mysql-jdbc-mm?

As for Kees Jan's port, rather than USE_ZIP it should probably
define:

  EXTRACT_CMD=	${JAVA_HOME}/bin/jar
  EXTRACT_BEFORE_ARGS=	-xf

It might even be nice if bsd.port.mk supported a USE_JAR macro.

The 'post-install'
rule also needs to add some cleanup rules to the TMPPLIST
(these are also missing from the current mysql-jdbc-mm port):

  post-install:
  .if !defined(NOPORTDOCS)
	@${MKDIR} ${JAVADOCDIR}
	@(cd ${WRKSRC} && \
		${JAVADOC} -d ${JAVADOCDIR} `find org -name \*.java -print`)
	@(cd ${PREFIX} && find ${DOCSUFFIX} -type f -print >> ${TMPPLIST})
	${ECHO} "@dirrm share/java/mysql-jdbc" >> ${TMPPLIST}
  .endif
	${ECHO} "@unexec ${RMDIR} %D/share/java/classes 2>/dev/null || true" >> ${TMPPLIST}
	${ECHO} "@unexec ${RMDIR} %D/share/java 2>/dev/null || true" >> ${TMPPLIST}
